entries.label: app-store-hero
entries.note: Drift-watch refresh 2026-07-13, triggered by app-store version bump to 16.110.3 (released 2026-07-07). Real Word-for-Mac ribbon/window chrome: Home tab active, ribbon groups (Paste/Font/Paragraph/Styles/Editing/Copilot), title bar with AutoSave+traffic lights+doc title+Comments/Editing/Share, status bar (Page 1 of 2, 145 words, language, accessibility). Staged marketing content ('Seattle Coffee Culture' doc) and a 3D-tilted product-shot presentation -- discount composition/perspective, trust chrome geometry/colors/typography/iconography. Version matches drift trigger (current 16.110.3, no previous dated screenshot set existed for this family to diff against).
entries.assetDecision: Ground-truth reference of the real application for fidelity work — recreate the surface as closely as possible from it. Logged-in captures may contain the owner's private content; keep those local and use synthetic demo content in tracked surfaces.

entries.label: app-store-review-editor
entries.note: Drift-watch refresh 2026-07-13, same App Store listing/version (16.110.3) as app-store-hero, filed same run. Flat (non-tilted) full window capture -- better for pixel/geometry comparison than the hero shot. Review tab ribbon active (Editor, Spelling & Grammar, Thesaurus, Word Count, Read Aloud, Check Accessibility, Translate/Language, comment controls, Track Changes, Accept/Reject/Compare, Block Authors/Protect Document/Always Open Read-Only/Restrict Permission, Hide Ink), open Editor sidebar (Editor Score 86%, Corrections: Spelling 11/Grammar 3, Refinements: Conciseness, Similarity, Insights), and a live spelling-correction flyout ('Cofee' misspelling with Coffee/Coffees/Cafe suggestions). Staged document content ('History of Coffee') -- discount; chrome/dialog/flyout geometry and color are real current-version UI.
entries.assetDecision: Ground-truth reference of the real application for fidelity work — recreate the surface as closely as possible from it. Logged-in captures may contain the owner's private content; keep those local and use synthetic demo content in tracked surfaces.
